content: Let's continue work based on #394, #710, and DESIGN §11.

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - Messages API for directly passing messages to Claude, Tool Runner (`client.beta.messages.tool_runner`) for running an agentic loop over your own tools, manual tool-use loops, Managed Agents for server-hosted agents with a managed sandbox, prompt caching, and general Anthropic SDK usage; (4) Claude Tag (Claude in Slack) - what it is, setting it up for a Slack workspace, `/install-slack-app`. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Glob, Grep, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

addedBlocks:
## github
The GitHub MCP Server provides tools to interact with GitHub platform.

Tool selection guidance:
	1. Use 'list_*' tools for broad, simple retrieval and pagination of all items of a type (e.g., all issues, all PRs, all branches) with basic filtering.
	2. Use 'search_*' tools for targeted queries with specific criteria, keywords, or complex filters (e.g., issues with certain text, PRs by author, code containing functions).

Context management:
	1. Use pagination whenever possible with batches of 5-10 items.
	2. Use minimal_output parameter set to true if the full information is not needed to accomplish a task.

Tool usage guidance:
	1. For 'search_*' tools: Use separate 'sort' and 'order' parameters if available for sorting results - do not include 'sort:' syntax in query strings. Query strings should contain only search criteria (e.g., 'org:google language:python'), not sorting instructions. Always call 'get_me' first to understand current user permissions and context. ## Issues

Check 'list_issue_types' first for organizations to use proper issue types. Use 'search_issues' before creating new issues to avoid duplicates. Always set 'state_reason' when closing issues. ## Pull Requests

PR review workflow: Always use 'pull_request_review_write' with method 'create' to create a pending review, then 'add_comment_to_pending_review' to add comments, and finally 'pull_request_review_write' with method 'submit_pending' to submit the review for complex reviews with line-specific comments.

Before creating a pull request, search for pull request templates in the repository. Template files are called pull_request_template.md or they're located in '.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE' directory. Use the template content to structure the PR description and then call create_pull_request tool.

attachment.content:
- session-start-hook: Creating and developing startup hooks for Claude Code on the web. Use when the user wants to set up a repository for Claude Code on the web, create a SessionStart hook to ensure their project can run tests and linters during web sessions.
- adopt-claudinite: Bootstrap Claudinite into a consuming repo — mount, hooks, checks, skills. Use when asked to bootstrap, adopt, or set up Claudinite, or to baseline a repo to pick up updates.
- authoring-agent-docs: How to write instruction files coding agents follow reliably. Use before writing or editing any Claude instruction doc — a project CLAUDE.md, a convention doc, a routine spec.
- bug-investigation: Method for investigating a bug and pinning down its root cause. Use when investigating a bug report, when a fix didn't hold or a bug recurs, or when a report doesn't reproduce against main.
- bump-version: Raise the project's version — a minor bump by default. Use when the owner says "bump version".
- engineering-practices: General software-engineering practices — naming by scope, single-source-of-truth with drift guards, GENERATED-file discipline, earning each dependency, verifying real platform behavior. Use before writing or editing code.
- file-placement: Where a file should live — the reference-distance metric, the high-reach code smell, and the mandated-location and test-location exemptions. Use before placing, moving, or renaming a file, or when reviewing where one lives.
- generate-project-instructions: Decompose a project into its pack facets (working-style class, technology, aspect, domain) and extract its working instructions into reusable canon packs plus the project's own local packs (.claudinite/local_packs/). Use when a project has no established working style yet, or it exhibits a facet no pack covers — during bootstrap of a fresh/empty repo, or on an uplevel request.
- prose-to-checks: Mine the corpus's existing prose (pack RULES.md, skill SKILL.md) for always-testable rules that were never converted to checks, and convert the strongest ones. Use when auditing the corpus for convertible rules, or when the nightly growth routine runs its prose-to-checks sweep.
- repo-text-sweeps: Mechanics for grep/sed sweeps, renames, and path relocations across a repo. Use before a bulk find-replace, a rename, or moving files — and after one, to catch silently broken references.
- unattended-agents: Architecture and practices for unattended, automation-invoked agents and recurring routines. Use when building, structuring, or running an AI agent, a scheduled routine, or a multi-stage agent pipeline.
- writing-claudinite-skills: Authoring or changing a skill in the Claudinite corpus — SKILL.md conventions, where a skill's checks and tests live, catalog and pack wiring. Use when creating or editing a pack's skills/<name>/ in the canon; a consumer project's own agent docs are authoring-agent-docs' turf.
- writing-tests: Practices for writing tests you can trust. Use before writing or changing any test — see-it-fail discipline, snapshot/golden rules, CI-only and heavy-browser tests, fuzzy-metric gating.
- add-live-case: Record a cached event page and author its reviewed live case for the extractor suite. Use when adding or refreshing a case under dev/requirements/extractor/ — new site support, a new behavior on a supported site, or re-recording a taken-down event page.
- merge-and-ci: Drive this repo's PR-to-merged flow cheaply — when to open the PR, how to get CI green in a Claude web session, and how to poll without wasting wall time or tokens. Use when merging a change, waiting on CI, or opening a PR that touches e2e/heavy/UI tests.
- single-branch-status: Assess one branch's landed status — merged, already-in-main, superseded, orphaned, or genuine unmerged work — read-only. Use when triaging whether a single branch is safe to delete, judging by content not the ref's name (e.g. the tidy-repo repo-tidy task hands you branches to assess).
- single-issue-triage: Triage one open issue and take the first applicable action — close-if-implemented, needs-decision, blocked, quick-win, or leave. Use when acting on a single issue; "implemented in main" must be verified against main's current content and the default is to comment, not close (e.g. the tidy-repo repo-tidy task hands you issues to triage).
- single-pr-status: Assess one open pull request's landed status — keep-open, or closeable (merged, already-in-main, superseded, stale) — read-only. Use when triaging whether a single PR should stay open, judging by its commits and diff not its title (e.g. the tidy-repo repo-tidy task hands you PRs to assess).
- snapshot-approval: Get owner approval for a moved UI-snapshot baseline. Use whenever a change to a dev/requirements case (its spec or its rendering) makes the snapshot tests fail — the pixels moved and the baseline wants to change.
- testing-guide: Map of this repo's test suites, the requirements model, and where each harness documents itself. Use before writing or changing tests here, adding a requirement case, or debugging a suite — the invariants live in the gcec pack's RULES.md; this is the deeper how-to.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact page can be granted — behavior static HTML cannot provide on its own, such as the page reading live or connected data, keeping state shared across viewers, or updating and republishing itself. Serves this user's live capability roster and the typed call definitions. Load it whenever the user asks for an artifact needing any such runtime behavior.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- morning: Render the user's morning brief as a styled HTML artifact, or set it up as a recurring weekday task. Use only when the user explicitly asks to run, see, or set up their morning brief, or if they invoke /morning by name. A question about their day, schedule, or calendar is not by itself a request for the brief; answer it directly instead.
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .xltx, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx or .potx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx or .potx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates (.potx), layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx or .potx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx or .potx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files) or Word templates (.dotx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', '.dotx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx or .dotx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
{"number":394,"title":"Implement per-project maintenance scheduling (owner-orchestrated rollout)","body":"Implementation tracker for the per-project scheduling conversion. Design + plan merged in #391 (Refs #390).

**Read first (on `main`):**
- Design — [`docs/per-project-scheduling/DESIGN.md`](https://github.com/missingbulb/Claudinite/blob/main/docs/per-project-scheduling/DESIGN.md)
- Migration plan — [`docs/per-project-scheduling/MIGRATION.md`](https://github.com/missingbulb/Claudinite/blob/main/docs/per-project-scheduling/MIGRATION.md)

**End state (DESIGN §8):** every repo schedules itself via a vendored hourly scheduler Action (the repo's only cron; hashed minute :10–:50) that evaluates per-task code preconditions and dispatches agent work as `[claudinite-task]` issues labeled `ready-for-agent`; a per-repo executor CCR routine fired by that label event runs each task at its declared model. `run_daily/` → `tasks//{task.mjs,task.md}`; `.claudinite/local_packs/` → `.claudinite/local/packs/` (declaration token `local/`). Fleet-scoped work (promote, discover-packs, migrations-retire, prose-to-checks, census) becomes ordinary tasks of the canon/sheepdog repos; baselining+migrations-apply become a per-repo basics self-refresh.

**Orchestration rules:** the owner drives each phase in a session; this rollout does **not** go through the regular migration-notes mechanism; the old central routine + the two GCEC triggers stay live (and are the rollback) until Phase 4; the `schedule` key in `.claudinite-checks.json` is the per-repo cutover marker, so exactly one mechanism owns a repo at any time.

**Phases (detail in MIGRATION.md):**
- [x] **Phase 0 — canon groundwork:** build `engine/scheduler/`; add `schedule` to `CONFIG_KEYS`; `local_packs`→`local/packs` (dual-root scan) + rename the canon's own; convert canon pack tasks (baselining → per-repo self-refresh, store-release → absorbs the release cron); new/rescoped checks; doc rewrites; the `schedule`-key transition guard. No behavior change until a repo declares `schedule`.
- [x] **Phase 1 — GCEC pilot:** vendor-refresh, scheduler workflow, strip the release cron, labels, move both `dev/routines/` into the gcec pack as `tasks/`, port the create-extractor triage *decision* into the precondition (fetch/scaffold stay in the task), write the `schedule` key, create the label-wired executor routine, disable the two old GCEC triggers. Soak ~1 week.
- [ ] **Phase 2 — canon cutover:** canon-side tasks (promote `daily`, discover-packs `weekly`, migrations-retire `daily+1h`, prose-to-checks `daily`), `fleet` signal over `FLEET_GITHUB_TOKEN`, sheepdog `fleet-census` task; canon repo gets its own scheduler + executor.
- [ ] **Phase 3 — remaining 9 consumers** (EdFringeNow, gRatio, TLDR, HelloWorldFlutterApp, EdFringeAllocator, CrosswordChat, Sheepdog, LaughCounter, ShoutsAndWhispers), batched ~3 at a time.
- [ ] **Phase 4 — decommission:** delete the central CCR trigger + both disabled GCEC triggers; remove `routines/auto-all-repos-maintenance.md` + `routines/fleet/`; drop the transition guard, `run_daily`/`local_packs` aliases; retire enrollment; final boxed review vs DESIGN §8.

**Independent fix (ship anytime, not gated on a phase):** `engine/pack_loader/inject-pack-prose.mjs` imports `registry.mjs` but the file is `pack-registry.mjs` — active packs' RULES.md are silently not injected at SessionStart, fleet-wide. One-line fix + regression test + vendor refresh.

content.text:
[{"id":5059452971,"body":"### Mid-rollout addition: task **pre-agent preprocessing** (owner-requested, 2026-07-23)

A new scheduler capability was added mid-rollout: a task may declare an `agent_preprocessing` command the scheduler runs as a subprocess **before** any agent (deterministic code out of the agentic run), plus `agent_preprocessing_timeout` / `agent_execution_timeout` bounds. It also carries the path to **dropping the canon repo from consumer CCR sessions** (only baselining still needs canon; vendoring `migrations/` + Action-side converge close that).

- **Design record + full continuation guide:** [`docs/agent-preprocessing/DESIGN.md`](https://github.com/missingbulb/Claudinite/blob/claude/issue-394-next-steps-wtdv6a/docs/agent-preprocessing/DESIGN.md) — owner decisions in §10, and **§11 "Remaining work — start here"** is the self-sufficient pick-up point.
- **PR:** #411 (branch `claude/issue-394-next-steps-wtdv6a`). #405 was closed and its primitives absorbed here.

**Landed & pushed on #411** (all signed, 615 tests green, sweeps clean): the full mechanism (contract → subprocess staging + hard timeout → inline-path retirement + `store-release` proof → executor best-effort bound) and the three §7 primitives — the migration-record `agentic` flag, vendored `migrations/`, and `converge-wiring.mjs`.

**Remaining (a fresh session — fleet-critical, live-piloted):** E4 baselining converge-as-preprocessing (public-canon fetch + native git + `agenticMigrations` escalation with the stamp-coupling rule), E5 drop canon from the executor's CCR sources, and converting the **GCEC** repo's tasks to the new contract. Full steps, available primitives, and the git-free-tests / FD-pacing notes are in DESIGN §11.

To continue: open the branch, read `docs/agent-preprocessing/DESIGN.md` §11, and work down the "Remaining work" list — each item its own commit.

Relationship to the phases above: the contract is written to precede **Phase 2** (so those tasks use the final shape), and baselining's rework reconciles with the per-cycle maintenance branch in #407.

---

Pickup point unchanged: [`docs/agent-preprocessing/DESIGN.md` §11 "Remaining work — start here"](https://github.com/missingbulb/Claudinite/blob/main/docs/agent-preprocessing/DESIGN.md) (updated this session with everything below).

**Landed / merged**
- **E4** — baselining converge-as-preprocessing (native-git worker, conditional agentless-night hand-off) + **E5** docs (executor sources → project-only) → **merged** in #412.
- **GCEC task conversion** (`agent_execution_timeout` on `fallback-extractor-improvements`) → **merged** in missingbulb/GoogleCalendarEventCreator#711.
- Scheduler stub bumped to `checkout@v5`/`setup-node@v5`/node 24 (cleared the Node-20 deprecation warnings on the hourly scheduler).
- **#407** closed as superseded.

**Open PR #413** (branch `claude/agent-preprocessing-remaining-kymhh9`)
- 🐞 **Fleet-fix: `executor.md` never vendored** — root cause of the broken GCEC executor (#710). `compute-vendor-set` stripped all engine `*.md`, including the executor's operating instructions, so **every cut-over consumer's executor booted with no instructions**. Fixed via a `VENDORED_ENGINE_DOCS` whitelist + regression tests.
- 📋 **Check-fix subsumption audit** ([doc](https://github.com/missingbulb/Claudinite/blob/claude/agent-preprocessing-remaining-kymhh9/docs/agent-preprocessing/check-fix-subsumption-audit.md)) — concludes it's **safe to drive the common night to `null`**; the `check_the_world` gate covers all standing-repo judgment checks (the 8 work-scoped omissions judge a session's change a mechanical converge never makes). 3 rote-fix checks noted for later mechanization.
- E4 **partial pilot** done: converge path (public-canon clone → rootless tree → `apply-vendor-set` past the anti-rewind guard → converge-wiring → mechanical migrations → agentic-note/stamp-hold/escalation) validated locally against a real older-stamped target. Only the native-git `deliver()` (push/PR/auto-merge) needs a real Action-token run.

**Front-of-line next (for the next session)**
1. Merge #413 (lands the `executor.md` fleet-fix + audit).
2. **GCEC mount bootstrap** — deadlock-breaker: GCEC's broken executor can't run baselining to refresh itself, so after #413 merges, converge GCEC's `.claudinite/shared/` to the fixed canon head **out-of-band** (push a GCEC maintenance PR, not through the executor). That lands `executor.md` + the new baselining `worker.mjs` → executor works (the 6 stuck `ready-for-agent` dispatches drain) → baselining becomes the Action-side worker. This refresh **is** the real E4 pilot.
3. **E5 rollout** — after the bootstrap/pilot, re-create GCEC's executor routine **project-only** (keep canon in sources until then).

**Open owner decisions**
- Split the `executor.md` fleet-fix out of #413 into its own PR for a faster independent merge? (Currently bundled with the audit.)
- Once merged, proceed with the GCEC mount bootstrap?

Anti-rewind guards passed (stamped `b5103ea` is an ancestor of `61b90ee`).

The refresh (28 files, 192-file vendor set, 0 deletions, stamp `b5103ea → 61b90ee`) lands both load-bearing files into GCEC's mount: `engine/scheduler/executor.md` (the #710 fix) **and** `packs/basics/tasks/baselining/worker.mjs` + its primitives (vendored `migrations/`, `converge-wiring.mjs`, `preprocess.mjs`, the `run.mjs`/`task-contract.mjs`/`validate-dispatch.mjs` updates).

**On #712's merge:** the executor gains instructions → the 6 stuck dispatches (#703–#708) drain → baselining becomes the Action-side worker. This refresh **is** the start of the real E4 live pilot.

DESIGN §11 updated to match in #415.

**Front-of-line next → step 3 (E5):** after #712 merges and the pilot proves out, re-create GCEC's executor CCR routine **project-only** (keep canon in its sources until it baselines onto the new worker, then re-create project-only).

_Generated by [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)_","html_url":"https://github.com/missingbulb/Claudinite/issues/394#issuecomment-5060979795","user":{"login":"missingbulb","id":73882448,"profile_url":"https://github.com/missingbulb","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/73882448?v=4"},"author_association":"OWNER","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-07-23T16:41:15Z","updated_at":"2026-07-23T16:41:15Z"},{"id":5061144529,"body":"### Post-bootstrap: E4 pilot has a hard gate before it can `deliver()` (found this session)

Working the E4 pilot after the bootstrap surfaced a real blocker — the baselining `deliver()` **cannot run Action-side yet**:

1. **Not forceable via `workflow_dispatch`.** Baselining is `daily-2h`; `slots.mjs` only makes a slot due within `(lastSuccess, now]`, which the hourly scheduler already consumes. A manual dispatch prints `- no tasks due` (confirmed: [GCEC run 30019987993](https://github.com/missingbulb/GoogleCalendarEventCreator/actions/runs/30019987993)). It fires only in the natural 02:xx run, and only once the stamp is `>1d` old — first eligible **~2026-07-25** (the out-of-band bootstrap re-stamped GCEC to head today).
2. **Would 403 on permissions.** `worker.mjs` `deliver()` pushes the maintenance branch + opens/auto-merges a PR via the Action `GITHUB_TOKEN`, but `stubs/claudinite-scheduler.yml` grants only `contents: read`. With an explicit `permissions:` block, `contents` is read and `pull-requests` is `none` → push and PR both fail. `store-release` sidesteps this by delegating delivery to a write-perm workflow; E4's baselining pushes directly, and the stub was never provisioned for it.

**Owner design fork:** (a) widen the scheduler stub to `contents: write` + `pull-requests: write` (matches E4's direct-push design; widens the repo's only cron token fleet-wide; needs the repo setting *Allow GitHub Actions to create and approve pull requests* + auto-merge enabled), or (b) delegate delivery like `store-release` (keeps the scheduler read-only). Either way the first GCEC run needs the fix applied **out-of-band** to GCEC's `.github/workflows/claudinite-scheduler.yml` (same deadlock-break as the executor.md bootstrap).

Docs reconciled + this gate recorded in #416. The `executor.md`/bootstrap E5 prose already landed in #412; only the executor-routine **re-creation** (project-only) remains for E5, still gated on the pilot.

---

- **GCEC mount bootstrap** → `GoogleCalendarEventCreator#712` (merged). Executor + E4 worker now in GCEC's mount.
- **Dispatches draining (confirmed):** re-fired the `ready-for-agent` label; the executor claimed #708 (`agent-running`) and swept the backlog to **0 open `ready-for-agent`** (#703–#708 all picked up). Deadlock empirically broken.
- **Pilot `deliver()` permissions gap — fixed** (owner decision: widen the stub). Canon stub + blocking drift-guard → #416 (merged); GCEC's live workflow bumped out-of-band → `GoogleCalendarEventCreator#714` (merged). Docs (§9 project-only, §11 gate) reconciled.

**Remaining before the E4 pilot can go green (owner-only):**
1. GCEC repo settings: **Allow GitHub Actions to create and approve pull requests** + **Allow auto-merge** (I can't toggle these via API).
2. Then the next natural **02:xx baselining run** (first eligible ~**2026-07-25**, once the stamp ages >1d — it can't be forced via `workflow_dispatch`; slot math prints "no tasks due") exercises the real Action-side `deliver()`.

**Then E5:** re-create GCEC's executor CCR routine **project-only** (the `executor.md`/bootstrap prose already landed in #412; only the routine re-provisioning remains).

**Front-of-line next → step 3 (E5):** after #712 merges and the pilot proves out, re-create GCEC's executor CCR routine **project-only** (keep canon in its sources until it baselines onto the new worker, then re-create project-only).

Working the E4 pilot after the bootstrap surfaced a real blocker — the baselining `deliver()` **cannot run Action-side yet**:

1. **Not forceable via `workflow_dispatch`.** Baselining is `daily-2h`; `slots.mjs` only makes a slot due within `(lastSuccess, now]`, which the hourly scheduler already consumes. A manual dispatch prints `- no tasks due` (confirmed: [GCEC run 30019987993](https://github.com/missingbulb/GoogleCalendarEventCreator/actions/runs/30019987993)). It fires only in the natural 02:xx run, and only once the stamp is `>1d` old — first eligible **~2026-07-25** (the out-of-band bootstrap re-stamped GCEC to head today).
2. **Would 403 on permissions.** `worker.mjs` `deliver()` pushes the maintenance branch + opens/auto-merges a PR via the Action `GITHUB_TOKEN`, but `stubs/claudinite-scheduler.yml` grants only `contents: read`. With an explicit `permissions:` block, `contents` is read and `pull-requests` is `none` → push and PR both fail. `store-release` sidesteps this by delegating delivery to a write-perm workflow; E4's baselining pushes directly, and the stub was never provisioned for it.

**Owner design fork:** (a) widen the scheduler stub to `contents: write` + `pull-requests: write` (matches E4's direct-push design; widens the repo's only cron token fleet-wide; needs the repo setting *Allow GitHub Actions to create and approve pull requests* + auto-merge enabled), or (b) delegate delivery like `store-release` (keeps the scheduler read-only). Either way the first GCEC run needs the fix applied **out-of-band** to GCEC's `.github/workflows/claudinite-scheduler.yml` (same deadlock-break as the executor.md bootstrap).

Docs reconciled + this gate recorded in #416. The `executor.md`/bootstrap E5 prose already landed in #412; only the executor-routine **re-creation** (project-only) remains for E5, still gated on the pilot.

309	## 11. Open questions & absorbed work
310	
311	Tracked here now that #405 is closed (§8):
312	
313	- **`agentic`-flag mechanics on migration records** (from #405): a machine-readable
314	  flag (e.g. `agentic: { model, instructions }`) marking a note as needing the
315	  agent stage, plus a conformance check that a record describing member-side
316	  adaptation must carry it. How baselining's preprocessing reads it to decide
317	  whether to apply `ready-for-agent`.
318	- **Baselining's stamp/agentic coupling** (from #405, #329/#330): preprocessing
319	  advances the stamp only when every pending note is fully mechanical; the agent
320	  stage handles a flagged note and advances the stamp itself. Exact transaction
321	  boundary to be specified in the baselining rework.
322	- **`converge-wiring.mjs`** (from #405): the fresh-path wiring convergence as an
323	  idempotent script that bootstrap Part 6 *calls* (one source of truth, the drift
324	  guard) — a mechanical step baselining preprocessing runs.
325	- **The check-fix subsumption audit** (from #405, load-bearing): **DONE** —
326	  [`check-fix-subsumption-audit.md`](check-fix-subsumption-audit.md). Conclusion:
327	  it **is** safe to drive the common-night model toward `null`. Every check that
328	  judges standing repo state is world-scoped and so is caught by the
329	  `check_the_world` escalation gate; the 8 work-scoped checks the gate omits judge a
330	  *session's change* a mechanical converge never makes (no silent regression); only
331	  2 checks are deterministically subsumed today, and 3 world-scoped needs-judgment
332	  checks (`catalog-completeness`, `generated-merge-driver`, `cer/version-sync`) have
333	  rote fixes worth mechanizing later to reduce needless escalation.
334	- **Rollout sequencing** of the contract change vs. #407 vs. Phase 2 (§8) —
335	  ordering agreed in principle (#407 → contract → Phase 2), dates open.
336	
337	### Implementation status
338	
339	- **Increment 1 — the declaration contract (landed on this branch):** the three
340	  fields + runtime validation (`task-contract.mjs`), the static
341	  `task-declaration-shape` guard, the doc, and every agentic canon task carrying
342	  an `agent_execution_timeout`. Behaviour-preserving — no task yet declares
343	  `agent_preprocessing`, and the scheduler does not act on the new fields.
344	- **Increment 2a — the staging mechanism (landed):** `run.mjs` two-stage flow
345	  (`preprocess.mjs` subprocess spawn + hard SIGKILL timeout, failure → one open
346	  needs-human issue, agentful success → hand-off, §3).
347	- **Increment 2b — inline-path retirement + first proof (landed):** an agentless
348	  task now REQUIRES `agent_preprocessing` (contract + static check), the in-process
349	  inline worker path is removed, and `store-release` converts to a standalone
350	  subprocess worker (§4).
351	- **Increment 2c — executor best-effort bound (landed):** `validate-dispatch`
352	  returns `executionTimeout`; `executor.md` gives the subagent its "fail after N
353	  minutes" bound from the trusted task decl, never the issue body (§6).
354	- **§7-1 — the migration-record `agentic` flag (landed):** `migrationAgentic` /
355	  `agenticMigrations` in `migrations/registry.mjs` + the pack-independence record
356	  structured.
357	- **§7-2 — vendor `migrations/` into the mount (landed):** `compute-vendor-set.mjs`
358	  carries the applier + registry + records, so baselining reads notes locally.
359	- **§7-3 — `converge-wiring.mjs` (landed):** the deterministic fresh-path wiring
360	  convergence (scheduler workflow + hashed cron, settings hooks, retired-import
361	  removal), which bootstrap Part 6 and baselining both call — the single wiring
362	  source of truth.
363	- **E4 — baselining converge-as-preprocessing (landed):** `packs/basics/tasks/baselining/`
364	  gains a native-git `worker.mjs` (`agent_preprocessing`) that fetches PUBLIC canon
365	  directly, runs `apply-vendor-set` + `converge-wiring` + mechanical `migrations/apply`,
366	  holds the stamp for a pending `agenticMigrations()` note, and delivers the converge on
367	  a per-cycle maintenance branch (find-by-prefix; **supersedes #407**). The AGENT stage
368	  (`task.md`, now the residual only) is requested via the run.mjs **conditional hand-off**
369	  above — only on a pending agentic note or a converge left non-green by `check_the_world`
370	  (owner, 2026-07-23); every other night is agentless. Pure decision helpers are unit-tested;
371	  the native-git/clone/REST I/O is **live-pilot-gated (GCEC)** — not yet exercised in CI.
372	### Remaining work — start here (a fresh session picks up from this section)
373	
374	**E4 and the GCEC conversion have LANDED** (branch `claude/agent-preprocessing-remaining-kymhh9`,
375	session 2026-07-23). The **GCEC mount bootstrap is DELIVERED** as a maintenance PR
376	(`GoogleCalendarEventCreator#712`, session 2026-07-23) — the out-of-band vendor refresh that
377	lands `executor.md` + the E4 `worker.mjs` into GCEC's mount. On merge it drains the 6 stuck
378	dispatches (#703–#708) and starts the real E4 live pilot. What's left is **E5**, gated behind
379	that merge + the pilot proving out.
380	
381	- **E4 — baselining converge-as-preprocessing (LANDED).** `packs/basics/tasks/baselining/`
382	  now carries `worker.mjs` (native-git `agent_preprocessing`) + a rewritten `task.mjs`
383	  (agentic residual model + preprocessing/execution timeouts) + a rewritten `task.md`
384	  (the residual agent stage only). Owner decision (2026-07-23): **agentless common
385	  nights** via the run.mjs **conditional hand-off** (worker writes `CLAUDINITE_REQUEST_AGENT`
386	  only when a pending agentic note exists OR the converge left `check_the_world` non-green).
387	  **Supersedes #407** (native-git delivery carries its own maintenance-branch prefix / find-
388	  by-prefix). Pure decision helpers unit-tested. **Partial pilot done (2026-07-23):** against a
389	  real target stamped at an older ref, the public-canon clone (through the proxy, no token) →
390	  rootless tree → `apply-vendor-set` converged to HEAD **without tripping the anti-rewind
391	  guard**, `converge-wiring` rewrote the workflow + hooks, mechanical migrations were idempotent,
392	  and the agentic-note detection + stamp-hold + escalation decision were verified both ways
393	  (pending note → hold + escalate; no pending note → advance). **Residual:** the native-git
394	  `deliver()` (per-cycle branch push, PR create, auto-merge arm) needs one real Action-token
395	  run — the E5 gate. The check-fix subsumption
396	  audit ([`check-fix-subsumption-audit.md`](check-fix-subsumption-audit.md)) is **done** and
397	  clears the common-night model to go toward `null`; the escalation gate (`check_the_world`
398	  green + no pending agentic note) is the operative safety net now and after the flip.
399	- **GCEC task conversion (LANDED, its own repo/PR).** `missingbulb/GoogleCalendarEventCreator`,
400	  branch `claude/agent-preprocessing-remaining-kymhh9`: `fallback-extractor-improvements`
401	  gained `agent_execution_timeout`. No deterministic pre-step was split into
402	  `agent_preprocessing` — its baseline must be *seen* by the agent and preprocessing has no
403	  code→agent channel; the fetch/scaffold pre-step belongs to the still-legacy create-extractor
404	  routine (outside `tasks/`).
405	- **FLEET-FIX — `executor.md` was never vendored (LANDED in this branch/#413).** Root cause of
406	  GCEC's broken executor (`GoogleCalendarEventCreator#710`): `compute-vendor-set` stripped ALL
407	  engine `*.md`, including `engine/scheduler/executor.md` — the executor routine's operating
408	  instructions. So **every cut-over consumer's executor booted with no instructions and could
409	  drain nothing.** Fixed with a `VENDORED_ENGINE_DOCS` whitelist + regression tests. Baselining
410	  can't self-heal this (a refresh re-excludes the file), so the fix must **merge**, then each
411	  cut-over consumer's mount needs a one-time out-of-band refresh to pull it (below).
412	- **GCEC mount bootstrap (DELIVERED — `GoogleCalendarEventCreator#712`, deadlock-breaker).** GCEC
413	  was stuck: broken executor → can't run baselining → can't refresh its mount to *get* the fix.
414	  With #413 merged (canon head `61b90ee`), GCEC's `.claudinite/shared/` was converged to that
415	  fixed head **out-of-band** via `vendoring/apply-vendor-set.mjs` — a GCEC maintenance PR, **NOT**
416	  through the executor (the anti-rewind guards passed: stamped `b5103ea` is an ancestor of
417	  `61b90ee`). That single refresh (28 files, 192-file set, 0 deletions, stamp `b5103ea → 61b90ee`)
418	  lands `executor.md` **and** the new E4 baselining `worker.mjs` + its primitives (vendored
419	  `migrations/`, `converge-wiring.mjs`, `preprocess.mjs`, the `run.mjs`/`task-contract.mjs`/
420	  `validate-dispatch.mjs` updates). **On merge:** the executor has instructions and drains the 6
421	  stuck dispatches (#703–#708); baselining becomes the Action-side worker. This refresh **is** the
422	  start of the real E4 pilot.
423	- **E5 — drop canon from the executor CCR session (NEXT after #712 merges + the pilot proves out).**
424	  `executor.md` sources become the project alone; update bootstrap Part 6 and the executor-routine
425	  creation to provision a project-only environment. Keep canon in GCEC's executor sources until it
426	  baselines onto the new worker, THEN re-create project-only.
427	
428	**⚠️ PILOT GATE — the scheduler workflow can't `deliver()` yet (found 2026-07-23).** Two
429	independent facts block a real Action-side pilot run of baselining's `deliver()`:
430	
431	1. **Can't force it via `workflow_dispatch`.** Baselining is `daily-2h` (the 02:00 slot); the
432	   run-ledger due-slot math (`slots.mjs`) makes a slot due only when it falls in
433	   `(lastSuccess, now]`, and the hourly scheduler already consumes it. A manual dispatch prints
434	   `- no tasks due` (empirically confirmed, GCEC run 30019987993). So the pilot fires **only in
435	   the natural 02:xx run**, and only when the stamp is `> 1d` old — after the out-of-band
436	   bootstrap re-stamped GCEC to head (2026-07-23), the first eligible fire is ~2026-07-25 02:xx.
437	2. **`deliver()` would 403 on permissions.** `worker.mjs` `deliver()` `git push`es the
438	   maintenance branch and opens/auto-merges a PR using the Action `GITHUB_TOKEN`, but the
439	   vendored `stubs/claudinite-scheduler.yml` grants only `contents: read` (+ `issues: write`,
440	   `actions: read`). With an explicit `permissions:` block, `contents` is read and
441	   `pull-requests` defaults to `none` — the push and the PR both fail. This is a gap in the E4
442	   landing: `store-release` (the other scheduler-run worker) deliberately **delegates** delivery
443	   to a separate write-perm workflow to keep the scheduler read-only, whereas E4's baselining
444	   pushes **directly** and the stub was never provisioned for it.
445	
446	   **Resolved (owner, 2026-07-23): widen the scheduler stub.** The stub now grants
447	   `contents: write` + `pull-requests: write` (matches E4's direct-push design; the read-only
448	   invariant `store-release` preserves by delegating is dropped for the scheduler, which runs
449	   only trusted committed code — untrusted issue bodies go to the tokenless executor). A
450	   `scheduler-workflow-shape` drift-guard asserts both writes so a repo can't silently regress to
451	   read-only. Two owner-only steps remain per repo: the setting *Allow GitHub Actions to create
452	   and approve pull requests* and *Allow auto-merge*. And because the scheduler workflow file
453	   lives in `.github/workflows/` (converged by `converge-wiring`, delivered *by* baselining), the
454	   first GCEC run needs the write-perms bump applied **out-of-band** to GCEC's own workflow — the
455	   same deadlock-break as the executor.md bootstrap — before it can self-deliver.
456	
457	**Live-pilot checklist for E4 (owner, before E5):** once the gate above is resolved, run
458	baselining's `worker.mjs` against a real GCEC scheduler run — confirm the public canon clone, the
459	converge writes, the per-cycle maintenance branch/PR, auto-merge arming, the stamp-hold on the
460	pending `pack-independence` agentic note, and that a clean night is truly agentless (no
461	`ready-for-agent` filed).
462	**Partial pilot already done** (converge path validated locally against a real older-stamped
463	target; only the native-git `deliver()` needs a real Action-token run) — see the E4 bullet above.
464	
465	**Working notes.** Prefer git-free fs-fixture tests (see
466	`engine-tests/scheduler/converge-wiring.test.mjs`) over `makeRepo` where possible —
467	`makeRepo` does real git commits that hang and leak file descriptors if the signing MCP
468	degrades. Watch `ls /proc/542/fd | wc -l` (the env-manager) and pace full-suite runs
469	(~800 FDs each, cap 4096) to avoid re-triggering the outage the prior session hit.
470	Baseline sweeps: 0 blocking, 4 pre-existing file-placement advisories.

- **`agentic`-flag mechanics on migration records** (from #405): a machine-readable

  advances the stamp only when every pending note is fully mechanical; the agent

  it **is** safe to drive the common-night model toward `null`. Every check that
  judges standing repo state is world-scoped and so is caught by the
  `check_the_world` escalation gate; the 8 work-scoped checks the gate omits judge a
  *session's change* a mechanical converge never makes (no silent regression); only
  2 checks are deterministically subsumed today, and 3 world-scoped needs-judgment
  checks (`catalog-completeness`, `generated-merge-driver`, `cer/version-sync`) have
  rote fixes worth mechanizing later to reduce needless escalation.
- **Rollout sequencing** of the contract change vs. #407 vs. Phase 2 (§8) —
  ordering agreed in principle (#407 → contract → Phase 2), dates open.

### Implementation status

- **Increment 1 — the declaration contract (landed on this branch):** the three
  fields + runtime validation (`task-contract.mjs`), the static
  `task-declaration-shape` guard, the doc, and every agentic canon task carrying

  `agent_preprocessing`, and the scheduler does not act on the new fields.
- **Increment 2a — the staging mechanism (landed):** `run.mjs` two-stage flow

  inline worker path is removed, and `store-release` converts to a standalone
  subprocess worker (§4).
- **Increment 2c — executor best-effort bound (landed):** `validate-dispatch`
  returns `executionTimeout`; `executor.md` gives the subagent its "fail after N
  minutes" bound from the trusted task decl, never the issue body (§6).
- **§7-1 — the migration-record `agentic` flag (landed):** `migrationAgentic` /
  `agenticMigrations` in `migrations/registry.mjs` + the pack-independence record
  structured.
- **§7-2 — vendor `migrations/` into the mount (landed):** `compute-vendor-set.mjs`
  carries the applier + registry + records, so baselining reads notes locally.
- **§7-3 — `converge-wiring.mjs` (landed):** the deterministic fresh-path wiring
  convergence (scheduler workflow + hashed cron, settings hooks, retired-import
  removal), which bootstrap Part 6 and baselining both call — the single wiring
  source of truth.
- **E4 — baselining converge-as-preprocessing (landed):** `packs/basics/tasks/baselining/`
  gains a native-git `worker.mjs` (`agent_preprocessing`) that fetches PUBLIC canon
  directly, runs `apply-vendor-set` + `converge-wiring` + mechanical `migrations/apply`,
  holds the stamp for a pending `agenticMigrations()` note, and delivers the converge on
  a per-cycle maintenance branch (find-by-prefix; **supersedes #407**). The AGENT stage
  (`task.md`, now the residual only) is requested via the run.mjs **conditional hand-off**
  above — only on a pending agentic note or a converge left non-green by `check_the_world`
  (owner, 2026-07-23); every other night is agentless. Pure decision helpers are unit-tested;
  the native-git/clone/REST I/O is **live-pilot-gated (GCEC)** — not yet exercised in CI.
### Remaining work — start here (a fresh session picks up from this section)

**E4 and the GCEC conversion have LANDED** (branch `claude/agent-preprocessing-remaining-kymhh9`,
session 2026-07-23). The **GCEC mount bootstrap is DELIVERED** as a maintenance PR
(`GoogleCalendarEventCreator#712`, session 2026-07-23) — the out-of-band vendor refresh that
lands `executor.md` + the E4 `worker.mjs` into GCEC's mount. On merge it drains the 6 stuck
dispatches (#703–#708) and starts the real E4 live pilot. What's left is **E5**, gated behind
that merge + the pilot proving out.

- **E4 — baselining converge-as-preprocessing (LANDED).** `packs/basics/tasks/baselining/`
  now carries `worker.mjs` (native-git `agent_preprocessing`) + a rewritten `task.mjs`
  (agentic residual model + preprocessing/execution timeouts) + a rewritten `task.md`
  (the residual agent stage only). Owner decision (2026-07-23): **agentless common
  nights** via the run.mjs **conditional hand-off** (worker writes `CLAUDINITE_REQUEST_AGENT`
  only when a pending agentic note exists OR the converge left `check_the_world` non-green).
  **Supersedes #407** (native-git delivery carries its own maintenance-branch prefix / find-
  by-prefix). Pure decision helpers unit-tested. **Partial pilot done (2026-07-23):** against a
  real target stamped at an older ref, the public-canon clone (through the proxy, no token) →
  rootless tree → `apply-vendor-set` converged to HEAD **without tripping the anti-rewind
  guard**, `converge-wiring` rewrote the workflow + hooks, mechanical migrations were idempotent,
  and the agentic-note detection + stamp-hold + escalation decision were verified both ways
  (pending note → hold + escalate; no pending note → advance). **Residual:** the native-git
  `deliver()` (per-cycle branch push, PR create, auto-merge arm) needs one real Action-token
  run — the E5 gate. The check-fix subsumption
  audit ([`check-fix-subsumption-audit.md`](check-fix-subsumption-audit.md)) is **done** and
  clears the common-night model to go toward `null`; the escalation gate (`check_the_world`
  green + no pending agentic note) is the operative safety net now and after the flip.
- **GCEC task conversion (LANDED, its own repo/PR).** `missingbulb/GoogleCalendarEventCreator`,
  branch `claude/agent-preprocessing-remaining-kymhh9`: `fallback-extractor-improvements`
  gained `agent_execution_timeout`. No deterministic pre-step was split into
  `agent_preprocessing` — its baseline must be *seen* by the agent and preprocessing has no
  code→agent channel; the fetch/scaffold pre-step belongs to the still-legacy create-extractor
  routine (outside `tasks/`).
- **FLEET-FIX — `executor.md` was never vendored (LANDED in this branch/#413).** Root cause of
  GCEC's broken executor (`GoogleCalendarEventCreator#710`): `compute-vendor-set` stripped ALL
  engine `*.md`, including `engine/scheduler/executor.md` — the executor routine's operating
  instructions. So **every cut-over consumer's executor booted with no instructions and could
  drain nothing.** Fixed with a `VENDORED_ENGINE_DOCS` whitelist + regression tests. Baselining
  can't self-heal this (a refresh re-excludes the file), so the fix must **merge**, then each
  cut-over consumer's mount needs a one-time out-of-band refresh to pull it (below).
- **GCEC mount bootstrap (DELIVERED — `GoogleCalendarEventCreator#712`, deadlock-breaker).** GCEC
  was stuck: broken executor → can't run baselining → can't refresh its mount to *get* the fix.
  With #413 merged (canon head `61b90ee`), GCEC's `.claudinite/shared/` was converged to that
  fixed head **out-of-band** via `vendoring/apply-vendor-set.mjs` — a GCEC maintenance PR, **NOT**
  through the executor (the anti-rewind guards passed: stamped `b5103ea` is an ancestor of
  `61b90ee`). That single refresh (28 files, 192-file set, 0 deletions, stamp `b5103ea → 61b90ee`)
  lands `executor.md` **and** the new E4 baselining `worker.mjs` + its primitives (vendored
  `migrations/`, `converge-wiring.mjs`, `preprocess.mjs`, the `run.mjs`/`task-contract.mjs`/
  `validate-dispatch.mjs` updates). **On merge:** the executor has instructions and drains the 6
  stuck dispatches (#703–#708); baselining becomes the Action-side worker. This refresh **is** the
  start of the real E4 pilot.
- **E5 — drop canon from the executor CCR session (NEXT after #712 merges + the pilot proves out).**
  `executor.md` sources become the project alone; update bootstrap Part 6 and the executor-routine
  creation to provision a project-only environment. Keep canon in GCEC's executor sources until it
  baselines onto the new worker, THEN re-create project-only.

**⚠️ PILOT GATE — the scheduler workflow can't `deliver()` yet (found 2026-07-23).** Two
independent facts block a real Action-side pilot run of baselining's `deliver()`:

1. **Can't force it via `workflow_dispatch`.** Baselining is `daily-2h` (the 02:00 slot); the
   run-ledger due-slot math (`slots.mjs`) makes a slot due only when it falls in
   `(lastSuccess, now]`, and the hourly scheduler already consumes it. A manual dispatch prints
   `- no tasks due` (empirically confirmed, GCEC run 30019987993). So the pilot fires **only in
   the natural 02:xx run**, and only when the stamp is `> 1d` old — after the out-of-band
   bootstrap re-stamped GCEC to head (2026-07-23), the first eligible fire is ~2026-07-25 02:xx.
2. **`deliver()` would 403 on permissions.** `worker.mjs` `deliver()` `git push`es the
   maintenance branch and opens/auto-merges a PR using the Action `GITHUB_TOKEN`, but the
   vendored `stubs/claudinite-scheduler.yml` grants only `contents: read` (+ `issues: write`,
   `actions: read`). With an explicit `permissions:` block, `contents` is read and
   `pull-requests` defaults to `none` — the push and the PR both fail. This is a gap in the E4
   landing: `store-release` (the other scheduler-run worker) deliberately **delegates** delivery
   to a separate write-perm workflow to keep the scheduler read-only, whereas E4's baselining
   pushes **directly** and the stub was never provisioned for it.

   **Resolved (owner, 2026-07-23): widen the scheduler stub.** The stub now grants
   `contents: write` + `pull-requests: write` (matches E4's direct-push design; the read-only
   invariant `store-release` preserves by delegating is dropped for the scheduler, which runs
   only trusted committed code — untrusted issue bodies go to the tokenless executor). A
   `scheduler-workflow-shape` drift-guard asserts both writes so a repo can't silently regress to
   read-only. Two owner-only steps remain per repo: the setting *Allow GitHub Actions to create
   and approve pull requests* and *Allow auto-merge*. And because the scheduler workflow file
   lives in `.github/workflows/` (converged by `converge-wiring`, delivered *by* baselining), the
   first GCEC run needs the write-perms bump applied **out-of-band** to GCEC's own workflow — the
   same deadlock-break as the executor.md bootstrap — before it can self-deliver.

**Live-pilot checklist for E4 (owner, before E5):** once the gate above is resolved, run
baselining's `worker.mjs` against a real GCEC scheduler run — confirm the public canon clone, the
converge writes, the per-cycle maintenance branch/PR, auto-merge arming, the stamp-hold on the
pending `pack-independence` agentic note, and that a clean night is truly agentless (no
`ready-for-agent` filed).
**Partial pilot already done** (converge path validated locally against a real older-stamped
target; only the native-git `deliver()` needs a real Action-token run) — see the E4 bullet above.

**Working notes.** Prefer git-free fs-fixture tests (see
`engine-tests/scheduler/converge-wiring.test.mjs`) over `makeRepo` where possible —
`makeRepo` does real git commits that hang and leak file descriptors if the signing MCP
degrades. Watch `ls /proc/542/fd | wc -l` (the env-manager) and pace full-suite runs
(~800 FDs each, cap 4096) to avoid re-triggering the outage the prior session hit.
Baseline sweeps: 0 blocking, 4 pre-existing file-placement advisories.

{"issues":[{"number":709,"title":"Claudinite executor routine is missing — ready-for-agent dispatch issues pile up unexecuted","body":"A scheduled session fired with the prompt `Execute the Claudinite executor: .claudinite/shared/engine/scheduler/executor.md`, but that file does not exist in this repo's vendored canon:

- `.claudinite/shared/engine/scheduler/executor.md` — missing
- `.claudinite/shared/bootstrap.md` — also missing (referenced by `.claudinite/shared/packs/basics/tasks/baselining/task.md` as the source of "bootstrap Part 6", which is supposed to have wired the label-triggered executor and stamped `executor.md` in place)

The supporting mechanics the executor would rely on are all present and documented (`engine/scheduler/dispatch.mjs`, `validate-dispatch.mjs`, `verify-outcome.mjs`, `task-contract.mjs`), but the prose entry point that safely ties claiming → validating → running → verifying together is absent. Since this is vendored, read-only canon content (per this repo's `CLAUDE.md`: "The canon is consumed read-only; lessons are captured locally... Reconciling the local packs with the canon is Claudinite's responsibility, not this repo's"), a scheduled session should not freehand that logic itself.

**Effect:** the hourly `claudinite-scheduler.yml` workflow is correctly filing `[claudinite-task]` dispatch issues, but nothing is executing them. Currently open and unexecuted:

- #708 `gcec/fallback-extractor-improvements`
- #707 `tidy-repo/repo-tidy`
- #706 `product-wiki/wiki-growth`
- #705 `grow_with_claudinite/growth-extract`
- #704 `grow_with_claudinite/growth-dedup`
- #703 `grow_with_claudinite/conversation-extract`

This matches the exact scenario the `baselining` task's own check text warns about: "a repo whose executor routine was deleted keeps filing `ready-for-agent` dispatch issues that nothing ever runs."

**Suggested remediation:** re-run (or complete) this repo's Claudinite bootstrap/vendor-refresh so `bootstrap.md` and `executor.md` land from the canon, and confirm the label-wired trigger (the thing that's supposed to fire a session on the `ready-for-agent` label event) is actually configured for this repo. Until then, the 6 issues above will need manual attention or a one-off manual run of each task.","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"missingbulb"},"created_at":"2026-07-23T12:11:37Z","updated_at":"2026-07-23T12:11:37Z"},{"number":705,"title":"[claudinite-task] grow_with_claudinite/growth-extract d2026-07-23","body":".claudinite/shared/packs/grow_with_claudinite/tasks/growth-extract/task.md

When convenient: copy the full body of this repo's `.claudinite/shared/mount/environment-setup.sh` into the Claude Code web environment's **Setup script** field and rebuild.","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"missingbulb"},"labels":["quick-win"],"comments":1,"created_at":"2026-07-21T10:04:45Z","updated_at":"2026-07-23T17:01:05Z"},{"number":679,"title":"Proof of concept: move local capture into Claudinite local packs (.claudinite/local_packs/)","body":"Claudinite is gaining first-class **per-project packs**: a tracked `.claudinite/local_packs//` root inside each consumer, carrying the full pack surface — prose rules (`RULES.md`), checks, `run_daily` tasks, and skills — discovered and run by the same engine as canon packs (prose injection at SessionStart, checks at Stop/CI, skill mounting, the fleet daily planner).

This repo is the proof of concept: restructure the existing local capture surface (`dev/procedures/*.md` working docs, `dev/routines/*`) into two local packs so the owner can review the target shape as a folder structure instead of a design doc:

- `.claudinite/local_packs/gcec/` — the project's general working pack (rules prose, a first real conformance check, snapshot-approval / merge-and-ci / testing-guide skills)
- `.claudinite/local_packs/extractor-pipeline/` — the extractor domain pack (the create-extractor and auto-fallback-coverage routines as `run_daily` tasks, the add-live-case skill, pipeline rules)

The PoC branch intentionally lands **before** the Claudinite engine support (pack discovery second scan root, sync-hook preserve, planner integration, migration); it must not merge until that support ships upstream.","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"missingbulb"},"labels":["needs-decision"],"comments":3,"created_at":"2026-07-15T08:38:01Z","updated_at":"2026-07-23T17:01:02Z"},{"number":674,"title":"Analysis: how many dedicated extractors could the generic fallback replace? (the ctz gap is the real blocker)","body":"Captures an analysis run comparing every dedicated per-site source against the generic fallback extractor (`extension/event-extractors/extract-unsupported.js`) on the reviewed corpus, to answer: **which custom sources are redundant — i.e. the fallback already extracts an identical event?**

Method: for each `dev/requirements/extractor/expected/*.json` page, run `GCal.extract()` twice on the same cached HTML — once normally (custom), once with `GCal.sources` emptied (fallback) — and deep-compare the **full normalized events arrays** (every event × every field: `title`, `start`, `end`, `location`, `ctz`, `eventLengthInMinutes`, `description`). A source counts as retirable only when **all** of its cases match. This is the same twice-extract harness the fallback-coverage gate uses.

## Headline

| Definition | Retirable | Sources |
|---|--:|---|
| **Strict** — byte-identical events, `ctz` included | **2 / 22** | `seetickets.com`, `stubhub.com` |
| **Loose** — ignore `ctz`; `start`/`end` count as equal when the same absolute instant (the coverage gate's own rule) | **5 / 22** | + `bandsintown.com`, `dash.datadoghq.com`, `dice.fm` |

All five are JSON-LD / `og`-driven single/listing pages where the dedicated source adds nothing structural — the generic schema.org path already reconstructs them. (`bandsintown` only reached the loose set after #673 trimmed the redundant `"@ Venue"` title tail — before that its title also diverged.)

## The timezone (`ctz`) gap is the real blocker

The gap between "2 strict" and "5 loose" is **entirely `ctz`**, and `ctz` is the single field the fallback **structurally cannot emit**:

- In the coverage report `ctz` is **0% recovered** by the fallback (0 / 31 gradeable) — every dedicated source that hardcodes or derives a timezone diverges on it by construction.
- The fallback-coverage routine explicitly treats inventing a `ctz` as a **forbidden win**: a *wrong* timezone is worse than none, because `ctz` is what Google Calendar anchors the event's instant to.
- Two sources differ from the fallback on **`ctz` alone** on their primary event — `bandsintown.com` and `dash.datadoghq.com`/`dice.fm` (the latter two also have `start`/`end` that are byte-different but the *same instant*, i.e. the source localized to floating wall-clock via its `ctz`).

So "retirable under a loose metric" ≠ "safe to delete": dropping any of the three loose-only sources silently loses correct timezone anchoring. The strict answer (**2**) is the only one that's actually safe on the fields shipped to Calendar.

## Why the other 17 earn their keep

Real work the generic path can't do generically:

- **Listing / multi-performance enumeration** the fallback can't unfold: `ticketmaster.co.il` (3 nightly showings folded into one multi-instance event — fallback finds only 1), `cinema.co.il` (109- and 6-event listings → fallback gets 1), `livenation.de` (39 vs 4 grouped), `seatgeek.com` (7 → **0**, the events live in `__NEXT_DATA__` JSON).
- **Hardcoded venue / curated description**: `cinema.co.il` (fixed "סינמטק תל אביב…" venue), `tel-aviv.gov.il`, `thinkdrink.co.il`, `eventim.co.il` (fuller description than `og:description`).
- **Non-JSON-LD markup**: `edfringe.com` (data only in `__NEXT_DATA__`; the fallback's body-text match is a jsdom-only artifact, invisible to Chrome's `innerText`), `tabitisrael.co.il` (fallback finds **0** events).
- `visit.tel-aviv.gov.il` — fallback picks up garbage (`location: "Save"`, a scraped near-now timestamp).

### Method caveat
Retirability is judged on the **1–5 cached exemplars per host** — the reviewed contract, but thin. `seetickets`/`stubhub` each rest on a **single** cached page, so a site redesign could reintroduce a gap the dedicated source was insuring against.

## Where this leads (the ctz lever)

The highest-leverage improvement is **not** more field scraping — it's a *safe, generic* `ctz` signal. Candidates that would be legitimate (Chrome-visible, not invented):
- a timezone already present in the page's own JSON-LD (`startDate` offset, or an explicit `location.address` region) — derive `ctz` only when the page states it, never guess;
- an IANA zone embedded in structured data (some platforms ship one).

If a generic `ctz` could be recovered where the page actually declares it, the strict-retirable count would rise toward the loose count — and, more importantly, every retained source's `ctz`-only divergence would close, shrinking the dedicated-source surface area meaningfully. Until then, the dedicated sources are carrying timezone correctness that the fallback can't.

## Context
- Emerged alongside #673 (the `"@ Venue"` title trim), logged under the fallback-coverage routine tracker #366.
- Reproduce: run `GCal.extract()` twice per case (normal vs `GCal.sources = []`) and deep-compare the events arrays; the loose variant reuses `datesMatch`/`toInstant` from `dev/requirements/extractor/fallback/fallback-coverage.js`.","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"missingbulb"},"created_at":"2026-07-11T06:01:06Z","updated_at":"2026-07-11T06:01:06Z"},{"number":648,"title":"Add a Claudinite conformance-checks job to CI (backstop for the Stop hook)","body":"## Problem

This repo runs the Claudinite conformance checks only via the **Stop hook** (Claude Code sessions). CI (`.github/workflows/test.yml`) does **not** run `node .claudinite/checks/run.mjs`, so a violation introduced outside a session — a human edit on GitHub web, or any non-session change — isn't caught by CI.

This surfaced during #646: the bespoke `shared-constants` uber test used to run inside `npm test` (so GCEC CI guarded those 8 constants on every PR). Retiring it in favor of Claudinite's canonical `shared-constants` check moved that enforcement to the Stop hook only. The canonical check is strictly better, but without a CI job it isn't a CI-level gate anymore.

## Fix

Add a CI job that runs the Claudinite check sweep (`node .claudinite/checks/run.mjs`) on PRs/pushes — the "CI is the backstop" job from Claudinite's design that this repo never picked up. It restores CI coverage for the migrated constants **and** adds it for every other Claudinite universal/tech-pack check (reference integrity, workflow lints, squash-merge history, etc.) — a net gain over the pre-#646 state.

Note the sequencing detail the check runner already documents: CI must ensure `.claudinite/` is populated (Method B tarball sync or submodule) before running the sweep.

- Add the one-line category declaration + a soft (non-`@import`) pointer in `CLAUDE.md`.

Claudinite's template catalog has no template for this category (only `research-project`), so per the generator prompt the new doc is flagged as the **seed for a future `templates/browser-extension.md`** — authoring that shared template is a separate, owner-approved step (Claudinite templates/README.md, "Reverse — no template fits").","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"missingbulb"},"labels":["needs-decision"],"comments":2,"created_at":"2026-07-04T17:37:05Z","updated_at":"2026-07-17T00:40:12Z"},{"number":616,"title":"Generate the project's working-instructions doc (category: Chrome MV3 extension)","body":"Task: follow Claudinite's `templates/generate-project-instructions.md` — determine this project's category and generate its working-instructions doc, linking a matching category template from `.claudinite/templates/` if one exists.

- Link it from `CLAUDE.md` (soft link, not `@import`) so the docs-reachable gate passes.
- Note in the doc that it seeds the first `templates/.md` upstream (Claudinite-side work, out of scope here).","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"missingbulb"},"labels":["needs-decision"],"comments":2,"created_at":"2026-07-04T17:29:27Z","updated_at":"2026-07-17T00:40:10Z"},{"number":592,"title":"Superseded local instructions (optimize-procedures)","body":"Standing ledger for the **optimize-procedures** routine's down-path (direction 1).

When that routine prunes a local instruction from this repo because the shared **Claudinite** canon now covers it — and pushes the removal straight to `main` with no PR to review — it logs the removal here as a **dated comment**. Each entry records, for **every** instruction removed in that run:

1. the **full text** of the removed local instruction, and which local doc it came from;
2. the **full text** of the canon instruction that superseded it, and which canon doc (path) it now lives in.

So this issue answers, for any dropped local guidance, exactly what in the canon replaced it — the durable audit trail for an unreviewed push.

This is found **by title**, not by a hard-coded number: the routine opens it if missing and reopens it if it was closed while still producing output. It is distinct from the routine's run-log tracking issue (activity history) — this one is the removals ledger.

Spec: [`dev/procedures/general/auto-optimize-procedures.md`](https://github.com/missingbulb/GoogleCalendarEventCreator/blob/main/dev/procedures/general/auto-optimize-procedures.md) (direction 1, "Track every removal").","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"missingbulb"},"labels":["needs-decision"],"comments":1,"created_at":"2026-06-30T14:19:02Z","updated_at":"2026-07-06T00:14:57Z"},{"number":438,"title":"Add a periodic "edge-case review" routine for the UI requirements","body":"A recurring (daily, Opus, Claude Code) routine that periodically re-reads `docs/uiRequirements.md` and the popup rendering code and **hunts for edge cases the requirements don't yet pin** — then either generates a `_(TBD)_` placeholder requirement (with a provisional snapshot of current behavior) for review, or, where the behavior is settled, generates the missing test. This is a thinking pass that should run regularly so gaps surface instead of accumulating silently.

### Why
This came out of a manual review pass that immediately turned up several uncovered/under-specified edge cases:
- **4.2.3** — cross-month grouping: one event with a Jun 15 instance, a Jun 28→Jul 3 *spanning* instance, and a Jul 10 instance. Today the span groups by its start (June only); should it also surface under July? (now a `_(TBD)_`).
- **4.10** — a single instance spanning multiple months: chip shows the start day only; should a long span show a date range on the calendar chip? (now a `_(TBD)_`).
- **5.7.3** — a grouped all-day card's header renders location-only; should it read "All day · location"? (now a `_(TBD)_`).
- **4.9** — the snapshot only checked card ordering, not within-card showing ordering (the second half of the requirement). Fixed this pass.
- **4.4** — "clickable" can't be shown by a cursor in the static renderer; a DOM assertion (the element is a `` whose computed `cursor` is `pointer`) belongs in a unit test. Candidate work item for this routine.

### Mechanics to mirror
Follow the existing daily-routine pattern (thin launcher pointing at an in-repo doc; its own standing tracking issue logging each run as dated comments; opens a PR, never merges) — see `docs/claude/auto-lessons.md`, `docs/claude/auto-fallback-coverage.md`, `docs/claude/auto-branch-report.md`. Add a `docs/claude/auto-edge-cases.md` spec and a standing tracking issue when it's built.

The `_(TBD)_` requirement kind (placeholder, exempt from the snapshot bijection, rendered with a "TO BE DECIDED" banner + optional provisional snapshot) already exists as of #436, so this routine has a place to put its findings.

Refs #436","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"missingbulb"},"labels":["needs-decision"],"comments":1,"created_at":"2026-06-20T11:03:51Z","updated_at":"2026-07-23T17:01:12Z"},{"number":435,"title":"Faithfully verify the behavioral UI leaves (events-view-actions stubs the boundary)","body":"**Tracking issue — the `_(behavior)_` UI requirements are only stub-verified.** There is a loud banner saying so at the top of `docs/uiRequirements.md`.

The behavioral leaves `3.4`, `9.1`, `9.2`, `9.3` (click → open adjacent new tab → close popup) are verified by `test/unit/events-view-actions.test.js`, which **stubs `chrome.tabs.create`/`window.close`**. Per `docs/engineeringPractices.md`, a test that stubs the exact boundary the behavior crosses can't confirm the real effect — only that our code asked for it. A faithful (non-stub) verification of these leaves is still owed.

The approach is **TBD by the repo owner** (to be solved separately) — this issue just tracks the open gap.

### Done (no longer part of this issue)
- The per-leaf snapshot migration is **complete** as of #436: every render leaf now has its own minimal `req-` case with its image embedded inline under the requirement in `docs/uiRequirements.md`, and the coverage gate is a strict one-case-per-leaf bijection.

Refs #434, #429","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"missingbulb"},"labels":["needs-decision"],"comments":1,"created_at":"2026-06-20T09:43:28Z","updated_at":"2026-07-21T00:38:01Z"},{"number":430,"title":"Add a daily automated UI-test improvement routine (with its own doc + tracking issue)","body":"## Goal

Add a daily unattended Claude **Opus** routine that tries to improve the **UI snapshot tests** — the counterpart of the existing daily routines (lessons `#365`, fallback-coverage `#366`, branch-report `#399`), each of which keeps its instructions in a repo doc and logs runs to its own standing tracking issue.

This routine runs **fully offline** on a fresh clone (the sandbox can't fetch live pages or run Chrome) and, like the others, **most days correctly changes nothing**.

## What it looks for

A genuinely useful, *true* improvement to the UI test suite, e.g.:

- **Claim accuracy** — a case that claims a requirement its snapshot can't actually verify (behavioral/unreachable leaves, the class found in the audit issue this references). Surface or re-home it.
- **Coverage honesty** — a leaf parked on a snapshot that should instead be a unit test on the view logic.
- **Missing rendering cases** — a leaf requirement whose visible rendering isn't actually pinned by any PNG, or a real rendering variation no case exercises.
- **Case simplification** — an over-complex case that could be split/trimmed per `docs/claude/testing.md` ("keep integration/UI cases as simple as possible").

## Scope / guardrails

- **Offline only**; deterministic. Touches `test/ui/**`, `ui/views/**` tests, and `docs/uiRequirements.md` claims — never ships popup behavior changes.
- An intentional pixel change must follow the **expected-vs-actual approval gate** (`docs/claude/workflow.md`) — never auto-accept a moved snapshot.
- Red-before-green; full suite green; regenerate `test/ui/README.md` via `npm run refresh:ui`.
- **PR for review, never a merge.** No real improvement → no branch, no PR.

## Plumbing (match the existing routines)

- New spec doc `docs/claude/auto-ui-tests.md`, imported from `CLAUDE.md`, written as the routine's source of truth; the launcher is a thin pointer to it (`docs/agenticBestPractices.md`).
- Its own standing tracking issue (find **by title**, reopen if closed), logging each PR-producing run as a **dated comment** — one issue per routine, per `docs/claude/auto-lessons.md` / `docs/agenticBestPractices.md`.
- Reference the audit issue (the companion to this one) as the seed backlog of known UI-test gaps.","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"missingbulb"},"labels":["needs-decision"],"comments":1,"created_at":"2026-06-19T21:48:59Z","updated_at":"2026-07-23T17:01:16Z"},{"number":366,"title":"🤖 Auto-Improvements Tracker - Fallback Extractor Coverage","body":"Standing tracking issue / **self-improvement log** for the daily **generic fallback-extractor coverage** routine (a Claude Code routine, not a GitHub Actions workflow — so there's no `.github/workflows` entry; this issue is its standing log).

The routine tries to make the **generic** fallback extractor recover more of what the dedicated per-site sources get, measured by the fallback-coverage gate, and opens a **PR for review** only on a clean, generic, test-covered win (most runs correctly change nothing).

Each run that produces a PR logs it here as a new **dated comment** — not a sub-issue — and references this issue (`Refs #366`), so the routine's output accumulates in one scrollable feed for reviewing it and spotting when the routine itself needs tuning.

**Long-lived**, found **by title**: if it's ever **closed**, the routine **reopens it** when a run next needs logging.

Full spec: [`docs/claude/auto-fallback-coverage.md`](https://github.com/missingbulb/GoogleCalendarEventCreator/blob/main/docs/claude/auto-fallback-coverage.md). Replaces the single umbrella idea in #360 (closed): each daily routine gets its own log. (The "lessons" digest's is #365.)","state":"OPEN","user":{"login":"missingbulb"},"comments":10,"created_at":"2026-06-18T13:25:54Z","updated_at":"2026-07-23T17:14:52Z"}],"totalCount":13,"pageInfo":{"hasNextPage":false,"hasPreviousPage":false,"startCursor":"Y3Vyc29yOnYyOpK0MjAyNi0wNy0yM1QxMjoxMTozN1rPAAAAASePXZM=","endCursor":"Y3Vyc29yOnYyOpK0MjAyNi0wNi0xOFQxMzoyNTo1NFrPAAAAARe17og="}}
